channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: German authorities received tipoffs last year about the suspect in Christmas market attack
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — German authorities said they received tipoffs last year about the suspect in a car attack at a Christmas market in Magdeburg as more details emerged on Sunday about the five people killed.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PROBST and VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli strikes across Gaza kill at least 20 as Vatican envoy visits Christians in the territory
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli strikes across the Gaza Strip overnight and into Sunday killed at least 20 people, including five children, Palestinian medical officials said.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Stampedes in Nigeria leave at least 32 dead in a rush to get Christmas gifts
item.description: Nigerian police say the death toll from stampedes during two Christmas charity events has increased from 13 to 32.
item.dc:creator: Dyepkazah Shibayan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Two US Navy pilots shot down over Red Sea in apparent 'friendly fire' incident, US military says
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Two U.S. Navy pilots were shot down Sunday over the Red Sea in an apparent “friendly fire” incident, the U.S military said, marking the most serious incident to threaten troops in over a year of America targeting Yemen's Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Winter is hitting Gaza and many Palestinians have little protection from the cold
item.description: Nearly two million displaced Palestinians in Gaza are struggling to protect themselves from the coming winter.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa And Fatma Khaled, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The water was their livelihood. Now Thailand's sea nomads work to preserve a vanishing way of life
item.description: The Moken, a group of indigenous people from Thailand and Myanmar, once sailed freely between the archipelago of 800 islands off the coast of the two countries.
item.dc:creator: Huizhong Wu And Jintamas Saksornchai, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pope to skip outdoor Sunday prayer after catching cold days ahead of Christmas Eve, Day Masses
item.description: Pope Francis is suffering from a cold and will give the Sunday blessing ahead of a busy week that includes Christmas Eve and Day Masses and the start of the Holy Year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump: Panama will lose control of Canal if it continues to ‘rip-off’ US
item.description: President-elect Trump took issue with fees charged to use the Panama Canal, a vital shipping route, on Saturday night, claiming that he will demand oversight of the waterway be given back to the U.S. when he returns to office if it's not changed.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, The Hill

item.title: US military conducts successful airstrikes on Houthi rebel forces in Yemen
item.description: The U.S. military on Saturday said it conducted precision airstrikes on a missile storage site and a command-and-control center in Yemen against Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A rocket from Yemen strikes Tel Aviv, injuring 16, and Palestinians mourn a dozen children in Gaza
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — A rocket fired from Yemen hit an area of Tel Aviv overnight, leaving 16 people injured by shattered glass, the Israeli military said Saturday, days after Israeli airstrikes hit Houthi rebels who have been launching missiles in solidarity with Palestinians in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN and WAAFA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden offers condolences, cooperation with Germany after Christmas market attack
item.description: President Biden issued his condolences on Saturday over an attack on a Christmas market in Germany that left five dead and injured 200. “No community—and no family—should have to endure such a despicable and dark event, especially just days before a holiday of joy and peace,” Biden wrote.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, The Hill

item.title: What we know about the deadly Christmas market attack in Germany
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — Five people were killed, including a 9-year-old, after a Saudi doctor drove into a Christmas market teeming with holiday shoppers in the German city of Magdeburg, officials said Saturday, as people mourned the victims and their shaken sense of security.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: German Christmas market ramming is the latest attack to use vehicles as deadly weapons
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A car-ramming at a Christmas market in Germany, which police are treating as an attack, is the latest in a grim series of events in which vehicles have been used as deadly weapons.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US residents flock to Mexico bearing gifts for family members, but leery of security
item.description: That annual return has been tarnished in recent years by concerns about safety on Mexican roads, as drug cartels sow terror on towns along highways in central states like Guanajuato and Zacatecas, and in Michoacan and Guerrero to the west.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, WGN 9

item.title: At least 2 dead and 60 hurt after a car drives into a German Christmas market in a suspected attack
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — A car plowed into a busy outdoor Christmas market in the eastern German city of Magdeburg on Friday, killing at least two people and injuring at least 60 others in what authorities called a deliberate attack.
item.dc:creator: EBRAHIM NOROOZI, CHRIS STERN and GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Doctors Without Borders points to 'clear signs of ethnic cleansing' by Israel in Gaza
item.description: Doctors Without Borders said Israel is committing “clear signs of ethnic cleansing” in its war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, The Hill

item.title: Amazon Indigenous community wins latest stage of court battle for lost territory
item.description: An Indigenous community in the Peruvian Amazon is celebrating a legal victory in the latest stage of its attempt to take back claimed ancestral rainforests. The Puerto Franco community of the Kichwa tribe say their territory was stolen to form the Cordillera Azul National Park in 2001.
item.dc:creator: ED DAVEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Justin Trudeau looks set to lose power after key ally vows to topple him
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau looks set to lose power next year after NDP leader Jagmeet Singh said he would move to bring down the minority Liberal government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 dead, many injured after man drives into German Christmas market in suspected terror attack: report
item.description: A festive Christmas market in Madgeburg, Germany erupted into chaos after a car was driven into a group of people.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Universities warn international students to come back to campus before Trump inauguration
item.description: More than a dozen U.S. universities have issued guidance to their international students urging them to be back on campus before the Jan. 20 inauguration of President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: Malaysia resuming search for MH370 wreckage
item.description: Malaysian officials approved a third probe into the Indian Ocean with the goal of recovering wreckage from flight MH370, which disappeared more than a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Knife attack in Croatian school leaves 7-year-old dead, 6 people wounded, police say
item.description: A young male in Croatia's capital killed a 7-year-old girl and wounded a teacher and five other students in a knife attack at Precko Elementary School.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pentagon doubled the number of US troops in Syria 'months' ahead of Assad’s fall
item.description: The U.S. for months has had more than 2,000 troops inside Syria, more than double the 900 troops the Defense Department previously said are on the ground there, the Pentagon’s top spokesperson revealed Thursday. The increased force presence has “been going on for a while,” with press secretary Maj.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: 13 die as an Indian navy speedboat crashes into a passenger ferry off Mumbai
item.description: At least 13 people were killed after an Indian navy speedboat crashed into a ferry that was carrying more than 100 people; the ferry was on its way to a tourist destination off Mumbai.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blinken says 'pressure is on Hamas' to move forward with ceasefire in Gaza
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken said the pressure is on the Palestinian militant group Hamas to move forward with the ceasefire in the Gaza Strip, arguing the group’s support from its allies in the region has dwindled in recent months.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Putin says Russia ready to compromise with Trump on Ukraine war
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said he was ready to make a compromise regarding ending the war in Ukraine in possible talks with U.S. President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Frenchman found guilty in horrific rape trial that shocked the world
item.description: A Frenchman accused of drugging his wife of 50 years and inviting strangers over to rape her – while he filmed the assaults – was found guilty on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia says it has detained a suspect in the Moscow bombing that killed a senior general
item.description: Russia's security service said Wednesday it detained a citizen from Uzbekistan in the bombing that killed a senior general as he left his southeastern Moscow apartment — a bold assassination that was claimed by Ukraine's security service. Lt. Gen.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu says Israeli troops will occupy a buffer zone inside Syria for the foreseeable future
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Tuesday that Israeli forces will stay in a buffer zone on the Syrian border, seized after the ouster of Syria's President Bashar Assad, until another arrangement is in place “that ensures Israel's security.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Blast kills 2 Mexican soldiers and wounds 5 others in suspected booby trap by drug cartel
item.description: While approaching, they spotted three dismembered bodies near the encampment, which appeared to be abandoned. But as they drew closer, a buried device exploded and struck the soldiers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Canada's finance minister resigns as unpopular Trudeau faces biggest test of his political career
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau faced the biggest test of his political career after Finance Minister Chrystia Freeland, long one of his most powerful and loyal ministers, resigned from the Cabinet on Monday.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation
